questions.text: The correct statement is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Na+. is smaller than Na atom
options.explanation: Yes Na+ is smaller than Na because Na+ is formed when an electron is lost from the Na atom,Thus the effective nuclear charge increases bcz the number of protons exceeds the number of electrons . This results in bringing the valence shell a little closer to the nucleus because of a very strong nuclear pull.

options.text: Cl- is smaller than Cl atom
options.explanation: Cl- (chloride ion) is bigger than Cl atom.,because cl- had 18 protons and electrons. But cl atom had only 17 protons and electrons. Atomic size will increase with atomic number.

options.text: Cl- ion and Cl atom are equal in size.

questions.text: The benzene molecule contains:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Benzene is an organic chemical compound with the molecular formula C6H6. The benzene molecule is composed of six carbon atoms joined in a planar hexagonal ring with one hydrogen atom attached to each. Because it contains only carbon and hydrogen atoms, benzene is classed as a hydrocarbon.

questions.text: Hydrogen bond is the strongest between the molecules of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As the name implies, one of the atoms must be hydrogen, therefore we are looking for it to be bonded to the atom with the greatest electronegativity, which is fluorine. Thus hydrogen fluoride, HF has the strongest individual hydrogen bonds between its molecules.

questions.text: Linear shape is associated with which type of hybridization.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: sp Hybridization can explain the linear structure in molecules. In it, the 2s orbital and one of the 2p orbitals hybridize to form two sp orbitals, each consisting of 50% s and 50% p character. The front lobes face away from each other and form a straight line leaving a 180° angle between the two orbitals

questions.text: Tincal is a mineral of
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.text: Which one of these polymers is an addition polymer?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An addition polymer is a polymer that forms by simple linking of monomers without the co-generation of other products. Addition polymerization differs from condensation polymerization, which does co-generate a product, usually water. Neoprene, Teflon, Polyvinylchloride are examples of addition polymers.

questions.text: Which one is a non-typical transition element?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Zinc is not a transition metal because it forms only Zn2+ ions with all the 3d electrons present. Because Zn has 3d orbital fullfil by 10 electrons, they are paired. that's why they can't release this electrons only lose ns electron. SoZn is not transitional element.

questions.text: How many zones through which charge passes in a rotary kiln?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to the temperature of the materials in the kiln, the cement rotary kiln system can be divided into: drying zone, preheating zone, decomposition zone, exothermic reaction zone, burning zone and cooling zone. The drying zone is a short part at the raw material entrance.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a fatty acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The aliphatic monocarboxylic acids are commonly called fatty acids because higher members of this series such as palmitic acid and stearic acid etc. are obtained by the hydrolysis of fats and oils. Phthalic acid is an aromatic carboxylic acid hence it is not classified as fatty acid.
